result.summary: Neon exposes one authenticated REST API, one authenticated CLI, and one authenticated MCP server; no GraphQL surface was confirmed.

neon_api_key.label: Neon API key
neon_api_key.setup: Create it from the Neon console or via `npx neon@latest init`, which can mint a Neon API key for CLI/MCP setup. See the [API reference](https://neon.com/docs/reference/api-reference) and [CLI auth](https://neon.com/docs/cli/auth). Store it securely; the docs indicate it is used for API, CLI, and MCP connections.

neon_oauth.label: Neon OAuth app / OAuth login
neon_oauth.setup: Use Neon's OAuth-based login flow from the CLI when you don't want to use an API key. The easiest path is `neon login` (see [CLI auth](https://neon.com/docs/cli/auth)); for MCP connectors, the client can also use OAuth via the Neon MCP Server config generator. This yields a user-authorized Neon credential for developer integrations.
